hero.heading: Gapflow and OneDrive
hero.subheading: Automate file management and collaboration workflows in Microsoft's cloud

documentation.title: Getting Started with OneDrive
documentation.description: Connect OneDrive to Gapflow and learn how to automate file uploads, downloads, and folder management.

documentation.title: File Permission Management
documentation.description: Control access to files and folders programmatically with automated permission updates and sharing links.

documentation.title: Advanced File Operations
documentation.description: Master batch operations, version control, and metadata management for sophisticated file workflows.

tutorials.title: Automated Document Backup
tutorials.description: Build a system that automatically backs up important documents to OneDrive with version history and organization.

tutorials.title: Team Document Workflow
tutorials.description: Create a collaborative workflow where team members can upload, review, and approve documents with automated notifications.

tutorials.title: Automated Report Distribution
tutorials.description: Set up scheduled report generation that uploads to OneDrive, creates sharing links, and emails stakeholders.

faqs.question: How do I connect OneDrive?
faqs.answer: Sign in with your Microsoft account and authorize Gapflow. You can connect personal OneDrive or OneDrive for Business accounts.

faqs.question: Can I access shared files?
faqs.answer: Yes! Access files shared with you, shared folders, and SharePoint document libraries connected to OneDrive.

faqs.question: What file size limits exist?
faqs.answer: Personal accounts support files up to 250GB. OneDrive for Business supports up to 100GB per file depending on SharePoint configuration.

faqs.question: How do I manage permissions?
faqs.answer: Create sharing links with specific permissions (view/edit), set expiration dates, and manage access for individuals or groups.

faqs.question: Can I work with file versions?
faqs.answer: Yes! OneDrive maintains version history. You can access previous versions, restore files, and track changes over time.

faqs.question: What about rate limits?
faqs.answer: Microsoft Graph API has throttling limits. Gapflow manages these automatically with intelligent batching and retry logic.

testimonials.quote: Automated document backup to OneDrive gives us peace of mind. Everything's secure and organized automatically.

testimonials.quote: Team collaboration improved 50%. Document workflows with automatic notifications keep everyone in sync.

limits.title: Storage Capacity
limits.description: Storage limits vary by plan (5GB free, 1TB with Microsoft 365). Monitor usage and implement archival strategies for large volumes.

limits.title: File Size Limits
limits.description: Large file uploads may take time and can be interrupted. Use resumable uploads for files over 100MB for better reliability.

limits.title: API Throttling
limits.description: Microsoft Graph API has rate limits. Implement batching for bulk operations and respect retry-after headers.

limits.title: Sharing Limits
limits.description: Files and folders have limits on the number of sharing links and permissions. Plan your sharing strategy accordingly.
